name: Fish Broth with Oysters and Saffron
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak mung bean threads in hot water for 20 minutes. Drain and cut into 2-inch lengths. Set aside.
2. Split carrots, celery, and leeks lengthwise into halves. Clean, peel, and slice vegetables into 1/2-inch-thick slices.
3. Melt butter in a stockpot over medium-high heat. Add onions, carrots, leeks, parsley, garlic, bay leaves, and juniper berries. Toss to coat with butter.
4. When vegetables begin to sizzle, reduce heat to medium-low and cover. Cook, covered, stirring occasionally for about 10 minutes.
5. Add tomato to the stockpot and stir for 1 minute. Add fish bones, trimmings, and white wine. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. Add cold water to cover and raise heat to medium-high. Bring to a boil, skimming froth as it forms. Lower heat and simmer gently, uncovered, for 35-40 minutes.
7. Strain the mixture, pressing solids firmly to extract all liquid. Discard solids.
8. Season with salt to taste.
9. Heat fish broth in a saucepan. Steam clams separately in a small amount of broth until shells open; remove each shell as it opens.
10. Transfer clam broth into warm soup bowls and strain into the fish broth, avoiding grit at the bottom. Add oysters and cook gently over low heat, uncovered, until edges curl about 1 minute.
11. Place 2 oysters in each bowl. Ladle warm broth into bowls and add mung bean threads. Garnish with radicchio leaves and saffron before serving.
